_description: S4: Markdown image URL exfiltration heuristics. Two-tier suspicious key list (strong = 1 hit -> danger, weak = 1 hit -> warning since v1.9.0) plus a TWO-tier host allowlist: imageOnlyHosts (CDN-only, safe to suffix-match) and userContentHosts (exact-host only, subdomain takeover possible on these so we never suffix-match them). R12: value contents are never inspected; only query-string keys decide severity. v1.9.0: high-FP generic weak keys (data/text/msg/message) demoted to `extraGenericWeakKeys` and NOT counted in the live detector — they stay in the JSON for future contextual rules (e.g. when combined with other strong signals) so we don't lose the curated vocabulary.
_extraGenericWeakKeysDescription: Demoted in v1.9.0 alongside the weak>=1 threshold relax. Too generic to count standalone (data/text/msg/message appear constantly in benign CDN / analytics / signed URLs). Kept on disk for future contextual rules — DO NOT load these into WEAK_KEYS without a paired strong-signal gate.
_imageOnlyHostsDescription: Dedicated image / CDN hosts. Suffix-match allowed (foo.cdn.jsdelivr.net etc. is still a jsDelivr asset). NEVER add a 2LD bare host here — see userContentHosts for those.
_userContentHostsDescription: User-content sites where subdomain takeover or arbitrary-bucket attacks are possible. EXACT-host match only — `attacker.googleusercontent.com`, `<bucket>.storage.googleapis.com`, and `attacker.notion.so` must NOT short-circuit. If a service ever offers a guaranteed-safe subdomain prefix, add the exact subdomain (e.g. `gist.github.com`) — never the bare 2LD.
